mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and subsequently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This mechanism reflects the direct link between consumer spending and economic health.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer confidence and corporate profits, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is elevated and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank. This policy aims to cool the economy, increasing the cost of capital and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently delivers positive 20-day returns (average > 0.5%) during periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL's 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in housing starts signals renewed confidence in the economy and an increase in construction activity, which has broad positive spillover effects on related industries and consumer wealth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as post-recession recovery, a 3-month average growth in HOUST above 5% does not lead to an average positive S&P 500 60-day return, the hypothesis is false.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and mining activity, reflecting healthy supply-side conditions and business investment, which typically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows negative average 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO's 1-month growth exceeds 0.5%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, leading to increased household income and consumer spending, which provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with the month-over-month growth of PAYEMS over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and low unemployment indicates an economy operating near full capacity with robust demand. This environment fosters strong corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, during periods where GDPC1 1-year growth exceeds 3% and UNRATE is below 5%, the S&P 500's average 60-day forward return is negative, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the Federal Funds Rate signal an aggressive tightening of monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampens economic activity, and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, this hypothesis is rejected.
